name: Sweet and Sour Fish Fillets
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice ocean perch fillets into pieces about 2x1".
Dredge in corn starch and allow to dry slightly on wax paper.
Make a batter of about 1/2 cup flour, 1 egg, 1/2 cup water,
and salt.
2. In a separate small pot, dissolve 1/3 cup brown sugar
and 1/3 cup cider vinegar over medium heat. Stir in corn starch dissolved in 1/2 cup water and add to the pot. Stir thoroughly. Add pineapple, carrot, sweet pickles, and tomato. Cook 3-5 minutes until sauce is clear and not cloudy. Reserve.
3. Pour oil halfway up a wok. Turn heat to high. When you think the oil is hot enough for deep frying, take a piece of bread crust and place in the oil to test. If the bread crust browns readily, then the oil is ready. If it takes longer than usual for the bread crust to brown or turns dark brown, turn off the oil and allow it to cool at least 5 minutes before using. Be sure to retest the oil before deep frying anything in it. When the oil is ready, place 8-10 pieces of fish into batter. Carefully lift pieces of fish from batter into the hot oil. The fish will initially sink to the bottom of the oil and then rise. Deep fry for about 4 minutes total (2 minutes on each side). Remove fish to paper towels to drain excess oil, then arrange on a serving platter. Repeat until all fish is deep fried. Pour sweet and sour sauce over fish.
NOTE: Shrimp or chicken are good substitutes for the fish in this recipe.
